items.title: The tech-broification of American science has officially begun
items.summary: The Trump administration unveiled the first "Genesis Mission" grants on Thursday, directing $5 billion toward hundreds of AI-driven science projects in an effort the White House has described as "comparable in urgency and ambition to the Manhattan Project." At roughly the same time, Trump's science adviser Michael Kratsios was on Capitol Hill selling lawmakers on […]

items.title: Black Friday in July sale drops Amazon’s full-size Echo Show 15 and 21 smart displays to their lowest prices
items.summary: As part of its ongoing Black Friday in July sale event, Best Buy is offering both the larger Echo Show 15 and Echo Show 21 smart displays at their lowest prices starting at $249.99 shipped. These models recently saw notable offers during Amazon’s Prime Day sale, and they’re both back to those prices from last month.

items.summary: Palestinians say villagers came under attack by settlers, while the Israeli military says a Palestinian stole a weapon and opened fire on hikers.

items.title: Best Buy’s Black Friday in July Sale offers up to 35% appliance savings across major brands starting from $135
items.summary: Best Buy’s Black Friday in July deals are in full swing through the upcoming weekend, and until Sunday night, you can score up to 35% savings across a wide array of major appliances from a variety of brands. One convenient and versatile inclusion is the LG 5.0 cubic-foot WashCombo All-in-One Electric Ventless Washer/Dryer with a FREE $100 e-gift card at $2,199.99 shipped, beating out LG’s direct website by $99 when factoring in the gift card addition. Normally $3,299 at full price, we’ve almost entirely seen this model discounted to $2,200 in 2026, though there were some earlier deals we spotted going as low as $2,099. While the price has gone lower in past years, you’re still getting a solid $1,099 markdown here for one of the best rates we’ve tracked in 2026. Head below to browse the collection of other appliance deals from multiple brands, or head to the main appliance sale page here.

Highlights include titles like Tokaido, Riptide GP: Renegade, Tower of Fortune 4, Roundguard, Dawnmaker, Golden Enigma, Charterstone: Digital Edition, and more. Everything is waiting down below.

There are a series of Switch 1 and 2 game deals in the ongoing Woot Summer Gaming sale, but we have also pulled some highlights from Amazon, Walmart, Best Buy, and elsewhere to browse through down below.

items.title: Delhi High Court rules OpenAI's use of ANI content to train ChatGPT wasn't copyright infringement as the news agency didn't show ChatGPT reproduced its reports (Munsif Vengattil/Reuters)
items.summary:
Munsif Vengattil / Reuters:
Delhi High Court rules OpenAI's use of ANI content to train ChatGPT wasn't copyright infringement as the news agency didn't show ChatGPT reproduced its reports  —  An Indian court said on Friday that OpenAI's use of news agency ANI's content to train its ChatGPT service did not amount to copyright infringement.

items.title: Anker’s flagship 14-port Prime dock with triple display support drops to $228 for Black Friday in July sale
items.summary: As part of the wider Black Friday in July sale, Anker’s official Amazon storefront is offering the brand’s 14-in-1 Prime Docking Station with display for $227.99 shipped. This is regularly a $300 multi-port hub, which is now seeing a $72 discount on its usual going rate with a 24% markdown. It’s now matching the lowest price of the year it first saw during the recent Prime Day sale, making this a great time to cash in and get one to upgrade your workstation. The Black Myth: Wukong limited edition variant of this docking station is also discounted to $269.99 shipped right now from its usual price of $340. Head below for more details.

Rumors and leaks have been flying since well before Nintendo made the remake official, and that special edition Zelda Switch 2 console is surfacing again, but it might not be the Ocarina of Time model.

items.title: Meta launches Facebook Verified, a free program it says will verify that users are real humans by analyzing a facial recognition selfie and assigning badges (Mat Smith/Engadget)
items.summary:
Mat Smith / Engadget:
Meta launches Facebook Verified, a free program it says will verify that users are real humans by analyzing a facial recognition selfie and assigning badges  —  And it's free.  —  In the face of AI content, profiles and slop, Meta hopes a new Facebook Verified program will help human users interact with other humans.

items.title: Beat the SSD price hikes with this deal on SanDisk’s 1TB portable drive at $135
items.summary: As part of its wider Birthday Leftovers sale event, Woot is offering SanDisk’s 1TB portable SSD for $134.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise). That’s $45 below the roughly $180 price it has recently been fetching at Amazon. We did spot an older version of this drive at $130 back in March, but the model on sale today ships with updated firmware to address some issues like the one that made the drive disconnect unexpectedly. That makes the extra $5 easy to justify, especially since discounts on SSDs have been rather rare these days. With SSD prices continuing to trend upward, this is one of the best prices we’ve tracked for the updated model, so be sure to snag it after getting more details below.

items.title: Best Buy offering FREE $200 gift cards with new Galaxy Fold 8/Ultra, and Flip, but there are much better deals elsewhere
items.summary: Pre-orders are now live on the new Galaxy Z Fold 8, Fold 8 Ultra, and Flip 8 with some seriously notable deals straight from Samsung – $100 off with code PAYPAL as well a FREE $200 Samsung credit that can net you a FREE pair of Galaxy Buds 4 and more. If you do prefer to grab your pre-order elsewhere though, you might want to avoid the Best Buy offers – it is offering notable trade-in values…but they are better straight from Samsung, and it is offering $200 gift cards…but Amazon is offering up to $350 gift cards.

Elevation Lab AirTag Fabric Extreme 4-pack

Buy for $32 (Reg. $40)

What we love

Whether you have some previous-generation AirTags to put to use, or just picked up the new AirTag 2 4-pack while at the all-time low, Elevation Lab is one of the best in the business when it comes to Apple item tracker accessories. The Fabric Extreme houses your tracker in a waterproof shell and discreetly affixes to the inside of jackets, bags, purses, and luggage.

items.title: Walmart has one of the best 4K-ready RTX 5070 Ti gaming PC deals right now at $601 off
items.summary: Best Buy’s Black Friday in July PC gaming deals are still going strong, but we just spotted one of the best deals on a gaming desktop at Walmart – MSI’s RTX 5070 Ti-powered gaming desktop for $1,699.99 shipped. That’s $600 off its usual $2,300 price and $49 below the $1,749 deal we highlighted just last month. The discount is particularly notable since gaming PCs equipped with an RTX 5070 Ti and 16GB of VRAM still commonly sell for well over $2,000, with many comparable systems landing closer to $2,500. This model also includes a Wi-Fi motherboard, a 1TB SSD, and bundled gaming peripherals. Head below for a closer look at the full specs.

items.summary: Alongside its ongoing Black Friday in July sale, and as part of its daily deals lineup, Best Buy is now offering Acer’s latest Chromebook Spin 514 model for $559 shipped, which is $190 off its usual $749 price. While that’s a touch higher than our previous mention from April, it’s still available at a solid price considering laptop prices are continuing to trend upward due to the ongoing memory shortages. Today’s deal lands this notebook within $80 of the $479 all-time low we first tracked earlier this year, and it remains a particularly good option for students shopping ahead of the back-to-school season.

items.title: LG Agrees To Remove Automatic Install of Adware From Monitors After Microsoft Steps In
items.summary:
LG has agreed to stop the automatic installation of adware, primarily that of McAfee pop-ups into the PCs of consumers without their consent, after Microsoft stepped in to deal with the situation. To put that into more localised terms: basically, Microsoft had to “turun padang” because LG messed up. Bad. Let’s recap what happened here: […]

The post LG Agrees To Remove Automatic Install of Adware From Monitors After Microsoft Steps In appeared first on Lowyat.NET.
